data.narrative:
Defense Unicorns is a Series B-stage defense and security company seeking valuation assessment against a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Heven AeroTech. The company has raised $186.5M to date against a last post-money valuation of $1.0B.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement to the $1.0B base, implying $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. The five peers cluster tightly: Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at $1.5B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, and Castelion and Heven AeroTech at $1.0B each.

The two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The stage-progression method sits above peer benchmarks, suggesting either above-market positioning or execution risk. The peer median provides a more conservative anchor. Key uncertainties include differentiation relative to the five core comparables and trajectory assumptions embedded in the stage-progression multiple. The $1.6B median valuation implies 18% upside from the current $1.0B post-money.
